name: ct-tree
description: Walk a directory for chosen file types and report the effective file tree with per-file line, word, and character counts (and on-disk byte size). Select files with --base/--name/--ext (--ext is a comma list of extensions added to --name as alternatives), filter with metric predicates (--min-lines/--max-lines/--min-words/--max-words/--min-chars/--max-chars/--min-bytes/--max-bytes) and per-folder predicates (--min-files-per-folder/--max-files-per-folder, counting matching files in a file's immediate directory), sort by path|name|lines|words|chars|bytes|ext (--desc for descending), and choose a summarisation level: --tree (indented tree with per-file counts and per-folder subtotals; default), --flat (one file per line: lines words chars path; best for ranked lists), or --summary (aggregates grouped by --group ext|dir|none). --bytes adds a byte-size column to the text modes (also enabled by --sort bytes). --json emits {tool, base, files:[{path,ext,lines,words,chars,bytes}], by_ext, totals}. Exit: 0 if any file is in the report, 1 if none, 2 on error. Read-only. Invoke as `ct tree ...` or `ct-tree ...`. Example: all *.rs files over 5000 lines sorted by line count descending = --ext rs --min-lines 5001 --flat --sort lines --desc.

base.description: Root directory to walk, relative or absolute. Default '.'.

name.description: File-name pattern; '|'-separated alternatives, each substring->glob->regex promoted and anchored to the whole name.

mode.description: Pin how --name/--ext patterns are interpreted; promotion off. Use literal for names whose characters would otherwise promote.

ext.description: Restrict to these extensions (no dots), e.g. ['rs','toml']. Added to --name as alternatives. May be comma-joined.

hidden.description: Include dot-entries (names starting with '.'). Default: skipped.

min-lines.description: Only include files with at least N lines.

max-lines.description: Only include files with at most N lines.

min-words.description: Only include files with at least N words.

max-words.description: Only include files with at most N words.

min-chars.description: Only include files with at least N characters.

max-chars.description: Only include files with at most N characters.

min-bytes.description: Only include files of at least N bytes.

max-bytes.description: Only include files of at most N bytes.

min-files-per-folder.description: Only include folders that directly contain at least N matching files.

max-files-per-folder.description: Only include folders that directly contain at most N matching files.

sort.description: Sort key. Default 'path'. In --flat the sort is global; in --tree it orders entries within each folder. Sorting by bytes also turns on the byte column. 'okf-type' sorts by a Markdown concept's frontmatter type (files without one sort first).

tree.description: Output mode: indented file tree with per-file counts and per-folder subtotals. This is the default mode.

flat.description: Output mode: one matching file per line with its counts. Mutually exclusive with the other modes.

summary.description: Output mode: aggregate counts only, grouped by --group. Mutually exclusive with the other modes.

bytes.description: Add a byte-size (on-disk) column after chars in the text modes. Independent of --min-bytes/--max-bytes; also enabled by --sort bytes. JSON always includes bytes.

group.description: Grouping for --summary: by extension (default), by immediate directory, by OKF frontmatter type (files without one group under '(none)'), or a single grand total.

json.description: Emit a structured JSON result instead of text.

timeout.description: Abort with exit 2 (and a one-line message) if the report exceeds SECS seconds (fractional allowed).

heartbeat.description: Print a liveness pulse every SECS seconds (fractional allowed) while the run is in progress.

heartbeat-emit.description: Heartbeat line template. Tokens: {ELAPSED} (whole seconds so far) {TOOL}. Default: "[{ELAPSED}s]".

heartbeat-to.description: Stream heartbeat pulses are written to. Default: stderr.

no-ignore.description: Walk gitignored / .ignore files too (the .git directory is always skipped); by default the walk skips what git would.

examples.why: Per-directory line and file totals for Rust sources in one table, instead of ls -R and wc -l pipelines.
List Rust files by descending line count, instead of wc -l on a find result piped to sort.
Surface Rust files over 500 lines that may want splitting.
